Continuation training on the same Question/Answer pairs and format used in co-training, with a response-only loss mask. Kept as a default stage and improved gradually, since it collapsed tiny models three times but SFT experiments are cheap and now run at every scale rung.

Stage four, a pipeline default. The pretrained model continues training on the same pairs and light Question/Answer format already used during pretrain co-training, computing loss only on the response span. It has degraded tiny models rather than improving them in the runs tested so far (node 30), but is retained as a deliberately cheap experiment surface rather than removed.

Finetuning continues from the pretrain checkpoint and trains on prompt/response pairs rendered with the same INSTRUCTION_PREFIX format as the co-trained pretraining data (not role control tokens, which was the original design and a confirmed cause of collapse before the redesign), masking the prompt so only the response contributes to the loss. Despite matching the format and using response-only loss, this stage collapsed the model into repetitive, low-grammar output in three independent runs (node 30), while the co-trained base pretrained model stayed fluent. The decision is to keep it in the default pipeline and improve it gradually rather than make it opt-in: it is cheap relative to data generation, the scale-world runner (node 43) exercises it at every rung so each run yields a report_sft at pico, nano, micro, and full, and the collapse evidence all predates the referent relaxation and content broadening, so its behavior on the current corpus is itself unverified. Candidate improvements to try through this surface include replaying pretraining data during finetuning to counter forgetting, and lighter-touch adaptation.
